questions.question: Given the premises: P is a prime number and Q is an odd number. Conclude: If P is a prime number, then Q is an odd number.
questions.hint: Use the definition of prime and odd numbers to justify your answer.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a valid argument using the rule of Detachment? (1) If it rains, then the ground will be wet. The ground is wet. (2) If I am hungry, then I will eat breakfast. I am going to the kitchen. (3) If a number is even, then it is divisible by 2. This number is even. (4) All numbers are integers. This number is an integer.
questions.hint: Explain why your chosen answer is valid.

questions.question: Let P, Q, and R be statements such that P is true, Q is false, and R is true. Which of the following statements can be logically inferred from the above premises? (1) P → Q (2) P → R (3) Q → R (4) P ∧ R
questions.hint: Use a truth table to justify your answer.
